data.name: PasswordPolicyHelp
data.value: Use at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, and a number.

data.name: Password must be at least 8 characters.
data.value: Password must be at least 8 characters.

data.name: SettingsConcurrencyMessage
data.value: Concurrency conflict: the settings were modified by another user.

data.name: LeaveCountriesEmpty
data.value: Leave empty to make the method generally available.

data.name: ShippingRateTiersHelp
data.value: Rate tiers are evaluated by shipment mass and subtotal thresholds. Phase 1 keeps this as a simple admin-managed matrix without provider-specific live quoting.

data.name: ShippingMethodEditorIntro
data.value: Use this screen to manage carrier/service combinations and the rate tiers used by checkout and order shipping snapshots.

data.name: ShippingMethodConcurrencyMessage
data.value: Concurrency conflict. Reload and try again.

data.name: UsersPlaybookUnconfirmedScope
data.value: Use this queue for activation support and onboarding follow-up.

data.name: UsersPlaybookUnconfirmedAction
data.value: Open the user, then send the activation email or confirm the email directly only where policy allows.

data.name: UsersPlaybookUnconfirmedFollowUp
data.value: If repeated email failures happen, continue in Business Communications and Email Audits.

data.name: UsersPlaybookLockedScope
data.value: Treat lockouts as support and security events, not casual cleanup.

data.name: UsersPlaybookLockedAction
data.value: Open the user, validate the support context, then unlock only when the issue is resolved.

data.name: UsersPlaybookLockedFollowUp
data.value: If the lockout was triggered by security concerns, rotate the password or review recent auth flows.

data.name: UsersPlaybookMobileLinkedScope
data.value: These users are important for device, push, and loyalty-related support.

data.name: UsersPlaybookMobileLinkedAction
data.value: Use the user profile together with Mobile Operations and Loyalty workspaces when stale devices, missing push tokens, scan failures, or notification issues are reported.

data.name: UsersPlaybookMobileLinkedFollowUp
data.value: Cross-check device fleet health and loyalty activity before assuming the user profile itself is wrong.

data.name: BusinessStaffAccessBadgeIntro
data.value: This QR mirrors the phase-1 staff badge behavior in Darwin.Mobile.Business. It is a short-lived support preview only; server-side authorization remains the source of truth.

data.name: BusinessStaffAccessBadgeConstraintWarning
data.value: This badge can be previewed for support purposes, but the member still has an account or membership constraint that may block real business operations.

data.name: BusinessStaffAccessBadgePayloadNote
data.value: Use this only for diagnostics or internal support. The nonce and expiry change every refresh.

data.name: BusinessMemberLastActiveOwnerWarning
data.value: This member is currently the last active owner for the business. Demoting, deactivating, or removing this membership requires a FullAdmin override with a mandatory reason. The override will be audit-logged.

data.name: BusinessMemberControlledOwnerOverrideNote
data.value: Use this only when support must remove the final active owner and there is no safer recovery path.

data.name: BusinessMemberOverrideReasonPlaceholder
data.value: Explain why this last-owner override is necessary.

data.name: BusinessMemberAssignmentHelp
data.value: Assign an existing platform user here. If the owner or staff member does not yet have an account, use the business invitation workflow instead.

data.name: BusinessMemberAllowLastOwnerOverrideLabel
data.value: Allow controlled override for last active owner rule

data.name: BusinessMemberLastOwnerOverrideRequiredPlaceholder
data.value: Required when changing the last active owner.

data.name: UserChangeEmailWarning
data.value: Changing the email requires confirmation. The user cannot sign in until the new email is confirmed.

data.name: CurrentEmailReadonlyHelp
data.value: This is the current login email. It is read-only.

data.name: NewEmailHelp
data.value: The new login email address for this user.

data.name: DelegatedSupportRoleNote
data.value: the built-in business-support-admins role is intended for invitation and business-member support actions only. Approval, suspension, owner overrides, and business lifecycle changes remain FullAdmin-only.

data.name: MobilePlaybookMemberLifecycleScope
data.value: Use this lane when device incidents overlap with pending activation, locked members, or business-member onboarding gaps.

data.name: MobilePlaybookMemberLifecycleAction
data.value: Review the user account first, then continue in Loyalty or Mobile Operations depending on whether the issue is account state, scan activity, or device health.

data.name: SiteSettingsMobileBootstrapIntro
data.value: These settings define the bootstrap and session contract used by the mobile apps. Review Mobile Operations and mobile-linked users before treating an incident as app-only drift.

data.name: SiteSettingsBusinessAppHandoffsTitle
data.value: Business app legal and billing handoffs

data.name: SiteSettingsBusinessAppHandoffsIntro
data.value: These URLs are used by business-facing mobile flows that hand operators off to external legal or billing pages rather than executing those actions inside the app.

data.name: SiteSettingsBusinessAppHandoffsNote
data.value: Keep only platform-wide handoff URLs here. Validate the final brand, sender, and local business defaults from Business Setup before treating a tenant as go-live ready.

data.name: SiteSettingsBusinessAppManagementWebsiteHelp
data.value: Used by the business app subscription screen for plan and billing handoff.

data.name: SiteSettingsBusinessAppDeletionHelp
data.value: Used by the business app account-deletion handoff flow.

data.name: EmailChangeRequestedMessage
data.value: Email change requested. The user must confirm the new email before signing in.

data.name: PasswordResetEmailSendFailedMessage
data.value: Failed to send password reset email.

data.name: CommunicationOpsIntro
data.value: Operational visibility for communication readiness before full template and delivery-log tooling exists.

data.name: NoSmsWhatsAppActivityYet
data.value: No SMS or WhatsApp activity has been recorded yet.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationProfileIntro
data.value: Business-level communication readiness, current policy flags, and phase-1 operational implications.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationProfileChannelActivityNote
data.value: This keeps non-email delivery visible while investigating one business. It is intentionally scoped to current live SMS and WhatsApp families, especially phone verification and operator transport tests.

data.name: SearchOrdersPlaceholder
data.value: Search by order number or currency

data.name: ChannelAuditsSearchPlaceholder
data.value: Search by phone, provider, preview, or flow

data.name: TaxSnapshotNote
data.value: Gross totals remain the operational source of truth, while this snapshot exposes the net/tax split currently persisted on the order.

data.name: ReturnSupportNote
data.value: Phase-1 returns support uses existing shipment and refund records. Returned parcels can be triaged here, while restock and deeper RMA workflow remain later-phase work.

data.name: InventoryAllocationSubtitle
data.value: Allocate reserved stock and finalize picking

data.name: InventoryAllocationNote
data.value: When allocation succeeds, the order transitions to Shipped (idempotent).

data.name: ReturnedShipmentFollowUpOpen
data.value: Returned shipment follow-up is open on this order.

data.name: ReturnedShipmentFollowUpNote
data.value: Use the shipment tab to confirm carrier state, then record the refund from this order.

data.name: UseRefundRestockFollowUp
data.value: Use refund and restock follow-up from this order.

data.name: BusinessesIntro
data.value: Create and maintain merchant tenants, owner assignments, and operational readiness.

data.name: SearchBusinessesPlaceholder
data.value: Search by business or legal name

data.name: SearchInvoicesPlaceholder
data.value: Search by id, order, customer, payment

data.name: CrmPlatformFallbackCultureNote
data.value: Used when no linked-user locale is available for customer-level follow-up.

data.name: CrmVisibleLocaleFallbacksNote
data.value: Current page rows relying on platform locale fallback instead of a linked-user locale.

data.name: CrmVisibleLinkedUserLocalesNote
data.value: Current page rows where locale comes from the linked identity profile.

data.name: InvoicesQueueIntro
data.value: CRM and order-generated invoices in one operational queue.

data.name: InvoicesTaxPolicyNote
data.value: This is a phase-1 VAT support surface for invoice review. It exposes current tax defaults and issuer identity without claiming full tax-engine automation or compliance completion.

data.name: RefundQueueIntro
data.value: Cross-order refund review for finance support and Stripe-first post-payment follow-up.

data.name: BusinessCreateOwnerInvitationIssued
data.value: Business created and owner invitation sent.

data.name: BusinessCreateNextSteps
data.value: Business created. Next, add a primary location and assign an owner.

data.name: BusinessSupportCustomerReady
data.value: Provisioned for CRM and billing follow-up

data.name: BusinessSupportCustomerProvisioned
data.value: CRM support profile provisioned from business onboarding.

data.name: BusinessSupportCustomerUpdated
data.value: CRM support profile synced with the latest business onboarding data.

data.name: BusinessSupportCustomerProvisioningBlocked
data.value: CRM support profile could not be provisioned yet. Missing: {0}.

data.name: BusinessSupportCustomerProvisioningPending
data.value: Complete the business name and contact email first.

data.name: BusinessSubscriptionCancelAtPeriodEndUpdated
data.value: Subscription will cancel at period end.

data.name: BusinessSubscriptionCancelAtPeriodEndUpdateFailed
data.value: Failed to update subscription cancellation policy.

data.name: BusinessSupportQueueIntro
data.value: Unified onboarding, activation, invitation, and failed-email triage surface for support operators.

data.name: NoAttentionBusinessesQueued
data.value: No attention businesses are currently queued.

data.name: NoFailedEmailEventsQueued
data.value: No failed phase-1 email events are currently queued.

data.name: ApprovalChecklistRequirement
data.value: Approval and checklist must both be satisfied.

data.name: SetupIncompleteWarning
data.value: Setup is still incomplete. Finish owner assignment, primary location, legal name, and contact email before operational approval.

data.name: BusinessSetupLocalizationOwnershipIntro
data.value: Business setup owns the tenant-facing override for culture, currency, and time zone. Change these only when this business must differ from the platform fallback. If every tenant should inherit a new baseline, change Site Settings instead.

data.name: CustomerSegmentsIntro
data.value: Review CRM segmentation hygiene before campaigns, support filters, and reporting depend on it.

data.name: TaxPolicyComplianceScopeNote
data.value: Phase-1 exposes issuer/VAT readiness only. Archive and e-invoice workflows still require deeper compliance implementation.

data.name: RefundWorkflowNote
data.value: Use refund when captured funds must be returned. Use void only if funds were not collected.

data.name: LinkedUserOwnershipNote
data.value: When a linked user is selected, contact data should be treated as identity-owned.

data.name: CommunicationEmailAuditsPlaybookIntro
data.value: This queue now supports controlled retry only for live invitation, activation, and password-reset flows after safe target resolution. Everything else should still use the flow-specific actions below instead of blind replay.

data.name: CommunicationEmailRetryBlockedUnsupported
data.value: This flow still requires manual operator follow-up.

data.name: CommunicationEmailRetryBlockedClosed
data.value: Only failed or pending delivery rows can be retried.

data.name: CommunicationEmailRetryBlockedRateLimited
data.value: Retry limit reached: {0} attempts in the last 24 hours.

data.name: CommunicationEmailRetryBlockedCooldownUntil
data.value: Retry cooldown active until {0:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm} UTC.

data.name: CommunicationChannelActionBlockedCanonicalFlow
data.value: Do not replay historical verification messages. Request a fresh code through the canonical phone-verification flow.

data.name: CommunicationChannelActionBlockedCooldown
data.value: Wait for the transport cooldown window before rerunning the same diagnostic channel test.

data.name: CommunicationChannelActionBlockedUnsupported
data.value: No operator rerun path is defined for this non-email flow yet.

data.name: CommunicationChannelEscalationPhoneVerification
data.value: Repeated verification failures without any successful send. Review transport readiness and fallback policy, then escalate if the canonical flow is still blocked.

data.name: CommunicationChannelEscalationAdminTest
data.value: Repeated diagnostic transport failures without a successful send. Treat this as provider/config debt rather than another routine rerun.

data.name: CommunicationChannelProviderRecommendedVerificationElevated
data.value: Review SMS/WhatsApp readiness, fallback policy, and current verification channel choice before issuing another canonical verification code.

data.name: CommunicationChannelProviderRecommendedVerificationStable
data.value: Keep verification traffic on the canonical flow, then review recent provider failures before switching channel policy or escalating.

data.name: CommunicationChannelProviderRecommendedAdminTestElevated
data.value: Correct provider credentials, sender identity, or reserved test-target setup before rerunning more diagnostics on this lane.

data.name: CommunicationChannelProviderRecommendedAdminTestStable
data.value: Use the reserved test target for a controlled rerun only after checking provider config and template state.

data.name: CommunicationChannelProviderRecommendedGenericPending
data.value: Review the pending and failed traffic in this provider lane before taking another manual action.

data.name: CommunicationChannelProviderRecommendedGenericStable
data.value: Review recent failures in this provider lane before escalating.

data.name: CommunicationChannelProviderEscalationVerificationElevated
data.value: Escalate as provider or channel-policy instability if verification traffic continues to fail without any successful recovery in this provider lane.

data.name: CommunicationChannelProviderEscalationVerificationStable
data.value: Escalate only if the provider lane keeps degrading after readiness, fallback, and current phone verification path have been checked.

data.name: CommunicationChannelProviderEscalationAdminTestElevated
data.value: Escalate as provider/configuration debt when this diagnostic lane keeps failing without a successful send.

data.name: CommunicationChannelProviderEscalationAdminTestStable
data.value: Escalate only when repeated transport-test failures continue after configuration corrections.

data.name: CommunicationChannelProviderEscalationGenericElevated
data.value: Escalate this provider lane if failures keep accumulating without recovery.

data.name: CommunicationChannelProviderEscalationGenericStable
data.value: Escalate only if this provider lane continues degrading after basic transport checks.

data.name: CommunicationChannelChainRecommendedVerificationRecovered
data.value: Do not replay historical verification messages. If the user is still blocked, confirm the current phone number and request a fresh code through the canonical verification flow.

data.name: CommunicationChannelChainRecommendedVerificationBlocked
data.value: Do not replay historical verification messages. Confirm the current phone number, review preferred-vs-fallback channel policy, then request a fresh code through the canonical verification flow.

data.name: CommunicationChannelChainRecommendedAdminTest
data.value: Rerun diagnostics only to the reserved channel test target after correcting provider settings, templates, or channel policy.

data.name: CommunicationChannelChainRecommendedGenericPending
data.value: Review the latest pending or failed non-email attempts before taking manual action.

data.name: CommunicationChannelChainRecommendedGenericStable
data.value: Review recent non-email delivery history before escalating.

data.name: CommunicationChannelChainEscalationVerificationBlocked
data.value: Repeated verification failures without a successful send indicate a likely transport or channel-policy issue. Escalate after confirming SMS/WhatsApp readiness and fallback policy.

data.name: CommunicationChannelChainEscalationVerificationStable
data.value: Escalate only if the canonical verification flow keeps failing after channel readiness and policy have been checked.

data.name: CommunicationChannelChainEscalationAdminTestBlocked
data.value: Repeated admin-test failures without any successful send suggest provider/configuration debt. Escalate as transport setup or provider issue instead of repeatedly rerunning tests.

data.name: CommunicationChannelChainEscalationAdminTestStable
data.value: If a rerun succeeds, treat this as an isolated incident. Escalate only when repeated failures continue after config fixes.

data.name: CommunicationChannelChainEscalationGenericRecovered
data.value: Escalate only if the chain continues to fail after a previously successful path has been revalidated.

data.name: CommunicationChannelChainEscalationGenericBlocked
data.value: Escalate when the same non-email path fails repeatedly without a verified successful send.

data.name: RecentSmtpAttempts
data.value: Recent SMTP-based transactional delivery attempts and failures.

data.name: NonEmailDeliveryHistory
data.value: Operator-facing history for live non-email delivery families.

data.name: ChannelAuditPhoneVerificationNote
data.value: Canonical verification flow for Web and Mobile.

data.name: ChannelAuditIdentityLifecycleNote
data.value: Account activation and password reset failures usually need account-state review before transport-only triage.

data.name: CrmCustomersFootprintNote

data.comment: Summary note for pipeline context on the customers workspace.

data.comment: Summary note for recent CRM interactions on the customers workspace.

data.comment: Operational note for linked-user customer rows.

data.name: CrmCustomerNeedsSegmentationNote
data.value: Current page rows without any CRM segment membership.
data.comment: Operational note for customers needing segmentation.

data.name: CrmCustomerOpportunityContextNote
data.value: Customers already carrying active opportunity context.
data.comment: Operational note for customers with opportunities.

data.name: CrmCustomerBusinessProfileNote
data.value: Visible business-profile customers in this queue slice.
data.comment: Operational note for business-profile customer rows.

data.name: CrmCustomerMissingVatNote
data.value: Business customers still missing VAT data for finance-safe follow-up.
data.comment: Operational note for business customers missing VAT data.

data.name: CrmOperatorPlaybooks
data.comment: Heading for CRM operator playbooks.

data.name: CrmNextStep
data.comment: Label for the recommended next step in CRM playbooks.

data.name: CrmCustomerLocaleFallbackPlaybookTitle
data.comment: Playbook title for CRM customers relying on locale fallback.

data.name: CrmCustomerLocaleFallbackPlaybookScopeNote
data.value: Rows using platform fallback culture usually need a linked identity locale or an explicit CRM locale decision before follow-up gets automated.
data.comment: Scope note for locale fallback customer playbook.

data.name: CrmCustomerLocaleFallbackPlaybookAction
data.value: Review linked-user coverage first, then clean up customer locale assumptions before support and lifecycle messaging rely on them.
data.comment: Operator action for locale fallback customer playbook.

data.name: CrmCustomerMissingVatPlaybookTitle
data.comment: Playbook title for business customers missing VAT data.

data.name: CrmCustomerMissingVatPlaybookScopeNote
data.value: Business tax profiles without a VAT ID usually indicate incomplete invoicing and compliance readiness for CRM billing follow-up.
data.comment: Scope note for missing VAT playbook.

data.name: CrmCustomerMissingVatPlaybookAction
data.value: Confirm the tax profile, collect the VAT ID, or downgrade the customer to consumer before invoices and finance operations depend on it.
data.comment: Operator action for missing VAT playbook.

data.name: CrmCustomerUnsegmentedPlaybookTitle
data.comment: Playbook title for customers without segment membership.

data.name: CrmCustomerUnsegmentedPlaybookScopeNote
data.value: Customers without segment membership are harder to target in lifecycle, support, and retention workflows.
data.comment: Scope note for unsegmented customer playbook.

data.name: CrmCustomerUnsegmentedPlaybookAction
data.value: Assign the customer to an existing segment or review whether CRM taxonomy needs a new segment before campaigns and support playbooks branch on segmentation.
data.comment: Operator action for unsegmented customer playbook.

data.name: CustomerFollowUpWorkspace
data.comment: Heading for the customer follow-up action card in the CRM editor.

data.name: CustomerFollowUpWorkspaceNote
data.value: Open the most common CRM follow-up lanes for this customer without leaving the current editing context.
data.comment: Supporting note for the customer follow-up action card in the CRM editor.

data.name: ReviewSegmentation
data.comment: Action label for reviewing customer segmentation context.

data.comment: Supporting note for the lead follow-up action card in the CRM editor.

data.name: OpportunityFollowUpWorkspace
data.comment: Heading for the opportunity follow-up action card in the CRM editor.

data.comment: Supporting note for the opportunity follow-up action card in the CRM editor.

data.name: Qualified leads visible in this queue slice.
data.value: Qualified leads visible in this queue slice.

data.name: Rows that still need an explicit lead owner.
data.value: Rows that still need an explicit lead owner.

data.name: Leads not yet attached to a CRM customer record.
data.value: Leads not yet attached to a CRM customer record.

data.name: Leads already carrying linked customer context.
data.value: Leads already carrying linked customer context.

data.name: Visible leads with heavier interaction history needing review.
data.value: Visible leads with heavier interaction history needing review.

data.name: CrmLeadQualifiedPlaybookScopeNote
data.value: Qualified leads should either convert into customers quickly or be explicitly reclassified before the queue grows stale.

data.name: CrmLeadQualifiedPlaybookAction
data.value: Review ownership and customer linkage first, then convert the lead or push it back to a more realistic status.

data.name: CrmLeadUnassignedPlaybookScopeNote
data.value: Leads without owners usually drift because no operator is clearly accountable for next contact and conversion follow-up.

data.name: CrmLeadUnassignedPlaybookAction
data.value: Assign an owner before adding more interaction noise so the next follow-up is explicit.

data.name: CrmLeadUnconvertedPlaybookScopeNote
data.value: Leads without customer linkage block downstream opportunity, invoice, and retention workflows.

data.name: CrmLeadUnconvertedPlaybookAction
data.value: Convert the lead when the relationship is real, or clean up qualification assumptions before pipeline work builds on it.

data.name: Opportunity rows still active in the pipeline.
data.value: Opportunity rows still active in the pipeline.

data.name: Visible opportunities requiring near-term close-date review.
data.value: Visible opportunities requiring near-term close-date review.

data.name: High-value pipeline rows visible in the current slice.
data.value: High-value pipeline rows visible in the current slice.

data.name: Opportunities still missing an explicit owner.
data.value: Opportunities still missing an explicit owner.

data.name: Rows with heavier interaction trails that may need operator review.
data.value: Rows with heavier interaction trails that may need operator review.

data.name: CrmOpportunityClosingSoonPlaybookScopeNote
data.value: Near-term close dates need explicit review so the pipeline does not drift into silent misses.

data.name: CrmOpportunityClosingSoonPlaybookAction
data.value: Review the owner, latest interaction, and close-date realism before the opportunity ages past the expected window.

data.name: CrmOpportunityUnassignedPlaybookScopeNote
data.value: Pipeline rows without owners usually mean nobody is accountable for moving the deal forward.

data.name: CrmOpportunityUnassignedPlaybookAction
data.value: Assign an owner before adjusting stage or close date so responsibility is explicit.

data.name: CrmOpportunityHighInteractionPlaybookScopeNote
data.value: Heavy interaction trails often signal stalled negotiation, support confusion, or unclear next steps.

data.name: CrmOpportunityHighInteractionPlaybookAction
data.value: Read the recent interaction trail before adding more outreach, then tighten the next operator action.

data.name: ProductsWorkspaceIntro
data.value: Track catalog readiness, launch timing, and merchandising visibility from one workspace.

data.name: SearchProductsPlaceholder
data.value: Search by product name, slug, or SKU

data.name: ProductsOpsTotalNote
data.value: Total product footprint currently managed in the admin catalog.

data.name: ProductsOpsInactiveNote
data.value: Products not yet live in selling flows and still needing release review.

data.name: ProductsOpsHiddenNote
data.value: Products hidden from merchandising surfaces and needing visibility confirmation.

data.name: ProductsOpsScheduledNote
data.value: Products with launch windows that need timing, stock, and content alignment.

data.name: ProductsOpsSingleVariantNote
data.value: Products relying on one sellable variant and often needing pricing or inventory review.

data.name: ProductsOpsVisibilityWindowNote
data.value: Quick signal for how much of the catalog is currently visible to storefront operators.

data.name: ProductAtLeastOneTranslationRequired
data.value: At least one translation is required.

data.name: ProductAtLeastOneVariantRequired
data.value: At least one variant is required.

data.name: ProductPlaybookInactiveScope
data.value: Inactive products stay out of live selling flows even when content and inventory already exist.

data.name: ProductPlaybookInactiveAction
data.value: Review pricing, legal content, and stock readiness, then reactivate when the item is ready.

data.name: ProductPlaybookHiddenScope
data.value: Hidden products often indicate merchandising holds, launch timing, or accidental visibility loss.

data.name: ProductPlaybookHiddenAction
data.value: Confirm whether the product should remain hidden and restore visibility if the business expects it to appear.

data.name: ProductPlaybookScheduledScope
data.value: Publish windows create timing-sensitive catalog behavior around launches, campaigns, and seasonal stock.

data.name: ProductPlaybookScheduledAction
data.value: Verify the publish window still matches campaign timing and that inventory and content are aligned before the window opens.

data.name: ProductCreateIntro
data.value: Define translations, variants, and base merchandising data before publishing the product.

data.name: ProductEditIntro
data.value: Review translation, variant, and readiness state before changing live catalog data.

data.name: ProductEditorTranslationsNote
data.value: Check naming, slug quality, and SEO fields across supported storefront cultures.

data.name: ProductEditorVariantsNote
data.value: Validate SKU, pricing, and stock assumptions before exposing the product to selling flows.

data.name: ProductEditorReadinessNote
data.value: Use this as a quick signal for whether the product still needs catalog shaping before release.

data.name: ProductSlugHelp
data.value: URL-friendly identifier. Lowercase, digits, hyphens. Example: organic-apple-1kg.

data.name: ProductMetaDescriptionHelp
data.value: Short SEO description, usually around 155 characters.

data.name: ProductSkuHelp
data.value: Seller stock keeping unit used for catalog, inventory, and fulfillment coordination.

data.name: MinorUnitsHelp
data.value: Store prices as integer minor units, for example cents, to avoid floating-point errors.

data.name: CategoryAtLeastOneTranslationRequired
data.value: At least one translation is required.

data.name: CategoryConcurrencyConflict
data.value: Concurrency conflict: the record was modified by another user.

data.name: BrandsWorkspaceIntro
data.value: Track brand publishing, SEO readiness, and asset completeness before storefront exposure.

data.name: BrandsOpsTotalNote
data.value: Total brand footprint currently managed for navigation and merchandising.

data.name: BrandsOpsUnpublishedNote
data.value: Brands still excluded from storefront-facing brand navigation.

data.name: BrandsOpsMissingLogoNote
data.value: Brands without visual identity setup for merchandising surfaces.

data.name: BrandCreateIntro
data.value: Define translation and asset basics before exposing the brand in storefront navigation.

data.name: BrandEditIntro
data.value: Review slug, logo, and translation quality before changing live brand presentation.

data.name: BrandSlugHelp
data.value: Optional unique, culture-invariant slug used for brand landing pages.

data.name: BrandLogoHelp
data.value: Optional media-library identifier for brand logo presentation.

data.name: BrandDescriptionHelp
data.value: HTML content will be sanitized before persistence.

data.name: BrandPlaybookUnpublishedScope
data.value: Unpublished brands stay hidden from brand-driven navigation and merchandising flows.

data.name: BrandPlaybookUnpublishedAction
data.value: Review whether the brand should remain internal-only, then publish it once storefront exposure is intended.

data.name: BrandPlaybookMissingSlugScope
data.value: Brands without slugs are harder to use in SEO-aware landing pages and support handoffs.

data.name: BrandPlaybookMissingSlugAction
data.value: Open the brand editor and add a stable slug before marketing teams link to the brand publicly.

data.name: BrandPlaybookMissingLogoScope
data.value: Brands without logos create weak or inconsistent catalog presentation across admin and storefront surfaces.

data.name: BrandPlaybookMissingLogoAction
data.value: Attach a media-library logo if the business expects visual brand presentation.

data.name: CategoriesWorkspaceIntro
data.value: Maintain storefront navigation structure, publishing state, and tree coherence from one workspace.

data.name: CategoriesOpsTotalNote
data.value: Total category footprint currently managed for storefront navigation.

data.name: CategoriesOpsInactiveNote
data.value: Inactive categories that can silently remove catalog paths from active navigation.

data.name: CategoriesOpsUnpublishedNote
data.value: Categories whose structure is ready but still not visible to storefront users.

data.name: CategoriesOpsChildNote
data.value: Child categories that depend on stable parent assignment and sort order.

data.name: CategoryCreateIntro
data.value: Define hierarchy, translation, and navigation state before exposing the category tree.

data.name: CategoryEditIntro
data.value: Review tree placement, translation quality, and publication state before changing live navigation.

data.name: CategoryDescriptionHelp
data.value: Optional short text shown on category pages.

data.name: CategoryPlaybookInactiveScope
data.value: Inactive categories remove products from expected navigation and can look like missing catalog data.

data.name: CategoryPlaybookInactiveAction
data.value: Confirm whether the category is intentionally disabled, then reactivate it if it still belongs in active navigation.

data.name: CategoryPlaybookUnpublishedScope
data.value: Unpublished categories may block storefront visibility even when translations and products are already ready.

data.name: CategoryPlaybookUnpublishedAction
data.value: Confirm publication intent and publish when merchandising wants the structure live.

data.name: CategoryPlaybookChildScope
data.value: Child categories rely on valid parent structure and often need extra review during navigation refactors.

data.name: CategoryPlaybookChildAction
data.value: Review parent assignment and sort order so the catalog tree remains coherent for storefront and business users.

data.name: ShippingMethodsWorkspaceIntro
data.value: Review carrier coverage, rate completeness, and production readiness before storefront routing depends on them.

data.name: SearchShippingMethodsPlaceholder
data.value: Search by name, carrier, service, or countries

data.name: ShippingPlaybookMissingRatesScope
data.value: Methods without at least one rate are not storefront-ready.

data.name: ShippingPlaybookMissingRatesAction
data.value: Open the method, add at least one pricing tier, and verify currency and carrier metadata.

data.name: ShippingPlaybookGlobalCoverageScope
data.value: Blank country scope means the method applies broadly and deserves extra carrier review.

data.name: ShippingPlaybookGlobalCoverageAction
data.value: Confirm whether the method is intentionally global or whether country scoping still needs to be narrowed.

data.name: ShippingPlaybookDhlScope
data.value: Use this subset when carrier setup or service mapping is being validated.

data.name: ShippingPlaybookDhlAction
data.value: Review service names, rates, and whether the method should stay active for production routing.

data.name: CrmOverviewIntro
data.value: Track funnel health, open pipeline, and operator follow-up priorities from one CRM surface.

data.name: CrmOverviewPipelineNote
data.value: Use this as a quick signal for how much active commercial follow-up still needs operator attention.

data.name: CrmOverviewPlaybookQualifiedLeadsScope
data.value: Qualified leads that linger without conversion usually indicate missed follow-up or unclear ownership.

data.name: CrmOverviewPlaybookQualifiedLeadsAction
data.value: Open the leads queue, triage qualified rows, and tighten assignment before more outbound work is added.

data.name: CrmOverviewPlaybookPipelineScope
data.value: Open pipeline value without clear movement often signals stalled negotiation or missing next-step ownership.

data.name: CrmOverviewPlaybookPipelineAction
data.value: Review open opportunities and invoice risk together before escalation or revenue forecasting changes.

data.name: CrmInvoicesPlaybookTaxScope
data.value: Invoices become operationally risky when issuer and VAT policy are only partially configured.

data.name: CrmInvoicesPlaybookTaxAction
data.value: Review tax settings before posting or refunding invoices that depend on B2B or archive-ready tax data.

data.name: CrmInvoicesPlaybookCollectionsScope
data.value: Outstanding balances and unlinked payments create support churn and reconciliation ambiguity.

data.name: CrmInvoicesPlaybookCollectionsAction
data.value: Use invoice, customer, order, and payment handoffs together before changing status or posting refunds.

data.name: MobilePlaybookTransportScope
data.value: Business-mobile onboarding depends on bootstrap settings and transport readiness together, especially when activation or alert workflows stall.

data.name: LoyaltyCampaignPlaybookWindowAction
data.value: Review activation state and the UTC campaign window together before escalating a campaign timing issue.

data.name: LoyaltyScanPlaybookFailureScope
data.value: Expired and failed sessions usually point to timing, staff workflow, or mobile transport confusion rather than reward-catalog issues.

data.name: NoDeviceVersionData
data.value: No device version data reported yet.

data.name: LoyaltyCampaignPlaybookWindowScope
data.value: Scheduled and expired campaigns are the first place to inspect when support sees stale mobile banners or campaigns that never went live.

data.name: LoyaltyRewardTierPlaybookSelfScope
data.value: Self-redeemable tiers affect customer-facing behavior without staff mediation and should be reviewed before promotions or reward support cases.

data.name: LoyaltyScanPlaybookPendingScope
data.value: Pending sessions represent customer scans still waiting on business device action and should not sit stale during mobile support incidents.

data.name: LoyaltyScanPlaybookFailureAction
data.value: Inspect expiry pressure and failure reasons first, then move to mobile operations or staff process review.

data.name: LoyaltyScanPlaybookProviderScope
data.value: Some scan incidents overlap with verification or alert transport pressure, so support should separate device trouble from communication-lane trouble.

data.name: LoyaltyScanPlaybookProviderAction
data.value: Review mobile diagnostics and communication provider lanes together before changing loyalty rules or campaign setup.

data.name: LoyaltyRedemptionPlaybookPendingScope
data.value: Pending redemptions still depend on business-side confirmation and should be reviewed before customers are told the reward path is broken.

data.name: LoyaltyRedemptionPlaybookPendingAction
data.value: Confirm the linked scan and account context before manually intervening in reward fulfillment.

data.name: LoyaltyCampaignPlaybookPushAction
data.value: Prioritize push-enabled campaigns when checking rollout readiness so content, transport, and device diagnostics stay aligned.

data.name: LoyaltyCampaignPlaybookPushScope
data.value: Push-enabled campaigns increase delivery expectations across mobile operations and communications support.

data.name: LoyaltyAccountPlaybookZeroBalanceScope
data.value: Zero-balance accounts often need program, accrual, or campaign review before support treats them as product defects.

data.name: MobileOperationsIntro
data.value: Bootstrap, onboarding, loyalty, and communication dependencies that directly affect Darwin mobile apps.

data.name: LoyaltyRewardTierPlaybookCatalogScope
data.value: Missing descriptions or mixed discount/free-item tiers make campaign copy and support guidance harder to interpret consistently.

data.name: LoyaltyProgramPlaybookSpendAction
data.value: Validate spend-based accrual settings before investigating balance mismatches so loyalty and payment support stay aligned.

data.name: AdminDashboardIntro
data.value: Use this overview as the operational starting point for the back-office.

data.name: DashboardBusinessOperationsEmptyState
data.value: Seed or create at least one active business to unlock inventory and billing summaries.

data.name: LoyaltyOperationsIntro
data.value: Programs, accounts, redemptions, and scan troubleshooting.

data.name: SelectBusinessForLoyaltyMobile
data.value: Select an active business context to review loyalty activity and mobile-business support flows.

data.name: MobileOperationsDashboardIntro
data.value: Device fleet health and support remediation.

data.name: MobileOperationsSnapshotNote
data.value: This workspace is an operations snapshot. Member activation, invitation handling, loyalty remediation, and communication triage still continue from Businesses, Loyalty, Business Communications, and Site Settings.

data.name: ChannelAuditExactChainNote
data.value: This exact-chain view is meant for phone-verification and diagnostic incidents where one recipient/channel path matters more than the whole queue.

data.name: SiteSettingsTransactionalPolicyIntro
data.value: These controls affect the currently implemented invitation, activation, password-reset, and phone-verification communication flows.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationTransactionalPolicyNote
data.value: Current invitation, activation, password-reset, and phone-verification flows depend on this policy surface and its test targets.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationPhaseOneImplicationSubjectPrefix
data.value: Subject prefix applies to current SMTP-based invitation, activation, and password-reset subjects.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationPhaseOneImplicationTestInbox
data.value: Test inbox reroute redirects those emails for controlled validation and prepends the original recipient to the body.

data.name: SiteSettingsCommunicationTestSmsTemplatePlaceholder
data.value: Darwin SMS transport test requested by {requested_by} at {attempted_at_utc} UTC for {test_target}.

data.name: SiteSettingsCommunicationTestWhatsAppTemplatePlaceholder
data.value: Darwin WhatsApp transport test requested by {requested_by} at {attempted_at_utc} UTC for {test_target}.

data.name: SiteSettingsPhoneVerificationSmsTemplatePlaceholder
data.value: Your Darwin verification code is {token}. It expires at {expires_at_utc} UTC.

data.name: SiteSettingsPhoneVerificationWhatsAppTemplatePlaceholder
data.value: Confirm your Darwin mobile number with code {token}. It expires at {expires_at_utc} UTC.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationPhaseOneImplicationVerificationTargets
data.value: SMS and WhatsApp test targets are transport-validation controls, while phone verification now uses customizable text templates.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationPhaseOneImplicationOperationalControl
data.value: This is an operational control, not a reusable template-engine or retry-queue replacement.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationNoReadinessIssues
data.value: No immediate communication readiness issues are visible for this business.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationNoCurrentFlows
data.value: No currently relevant built-in flows were inferred for this business.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationCurrentFlowsNote
data.value: This is an operational implication view only. It does not claim a reusable template engine or full Communication Core delivery policy yet.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationOpenFullChannelAuditLogAction
data.value: Open Full SMS / WhatsApp Audit Log

data.name: BusinessCommunicationLiveChannelFamiliesNote
data.value: These are the current live non-email message families. Operators can see the effective template, token surface, target path, and policy boundary without waiting for one exact-chain incident to reconstruct that context.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationNoLiveChannelFamilies
data.value: No live SMS or WhatsApp message families are currently exposed.

data.name: EmailAuditsSearchPlaceholder
data.value: Search by recipient, subject, provider, flow, or business

data.name: EmailAuditsEmptyState
data.value: No email audits matched the selected filters.

data.name: BusinessMembersNoActiveOwnerWarning
data.value: This business currently has no active owner. Assign one before go-live.

data.name: BusinessMembersPendingActivationNote
data.value: Some members are still pending activation. Use the inline support actions below to resend activation or confirm the email manually when support policy allows it.

data.name: BusinessMembersSearchPlaceholder
data.value: Search by user name, email or role

data.name: BusinessInvitationsSearchPlaceholder
data.value: Search by email, role or status

data.name: BusinessInvitationsPlaybookOpenWhyItMatters
data.value: Open invitation rows are the first place to review onboarding friction before member activation or business-access support drifts wider.

data.name: BusinessInvitationsPlaybookOpenAction
data.value: Review pending and expired invitation rows first, then resend or revoke only after confirming the latest onboarding state.

data.name: BusinessInvitationsPlaybookPendingWhyItMatters
data.value: Pending invitations often need support follow-up before they silently turn into account-activation or owner-coverage gaps.

data.name: BusinessInvitationsPlaybookPendingAction
data.value: Start with still-pending invitations when onboarding progress is unclear, especially before escalating to broader merchant support.

data.name: BusinessInvitationsPlaybookExpiredWhyItMatters
data.value: Expired invitations usually indicate onboarding drift, stale recipient details, or unresolved operator follow-up.

data.name: BusinessInvitationsPlaybookExpiredAction
data.value: Review expired rows before creating fresh invites so the same recipient does not accumulate conflicting invitation history.

data.name: BusinessSetupMembersPreviewNoAttention
data.value: No members currently need activation or lockout support.

data.name: BusinessSetupInvitationsPreviewNoAttention
data.value: No pending or expired invitations require attention right now.

data.name: BusinessLocationsSearchPlaceholder
data.value: Search by name, city or postal code

data.name: BusinessLocationsPlaybookPrimaryWhyItMatters
data.value: Business-facing operational flows usually assume one clear primary location for default fulfillment and storefront context.

data.name: BusinessLocationsPlaybookPrimaryAction
data.value: Review whether the current primary location still represents the live business entry point before onboarding or go-live approval.

data.name: BusinessLocationsPlaybookMissingAddressWhyItMatters
data.value: Incomplete address data weakens shipping, invoicing, and public business visibility across admin and mobile workflows.

data.name: BusinessLocationsPlaybookMissingAddressAction
data.value: Open the location and complete street, city, and country before relying on the location for live operations.

data.name: BusinessLocationsPlaybookMissingCoordinatesWhyItMatters
data.value: Geo-dependent discovery, mapping, and future nearby-business experiences depend on a stored coordinate.

data.name: BusinessLocationsPlaybookMissingCoordinatesAction
data.value: Open the location and add coordinates when the business expects map-aware or proximity-aware experiences.

data.name: BusinessSetupPlatformDependenciesIntro
data.value: Some settings are still managed globally in phase 1. Business-specific provider configuration is planned, but not yet part of the domain model.

data.name: BusinessSetupPlatformDependenciesRule
data.value: Operator rule: if a change affects every business or any provider secret, threshold, tax issuer default, or admin route, keep it in Site Settings. If it is brand, sender identity, support contact, or local defaults for one tenant, keep it in Business Setup.

data.name: BusinessSetupSecurityMobileAccessNote
data.value: JWT/mobile bootstrap remain platform-level in phase 1 and affect business app sign-in, refresh, and QR/session behavior.

data.name: BusinessSetupCommunicationsDependencyNote
data.value: SMTP, SMS, WhatsApp, and admin alert routing are still global in phase 1 even when sender defaults are business-scoped.

data.name: BusinessSetupPaymentsShippingNote
data.value: Stripe-first and DHL-first provider settings still remain a planned phase-1 dependency and are not yet modeled as business-scoped setup categories.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationFollowUpBacklogNote
data.value: Recent failed or stale-pending rows that still need operator follow-up.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationHighSeverityRowsNote
data.value: Business-linked failures and stale pending rows that deserve first-pass triage.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationSlowDeliveriesNote
data.value: Completed deliveries that took longer than the current operator threshold.

data.name: BusinessCommunicationSearchPlaceholder
data.value: Search by business, legal name, support email, or sender

data.name: BusinessCommunicationEmptyState
data.value: No businesses matched the communication filters.

data.name: DashboardQuickLinksIntro
data.value: Jump directly to the most-used back-office areas.

data.name: MobilePlaybookLoyaltyScanScope
data.value: Scan-session failures often belong with device and staff workflow review, not only reward-catalog cleanup.

data.name: MobilePushTokenClearFailed
data.value: Push token could not be cleared.

data.name: MobilePlaybookLoyaltyScanAction
data.value: Open scan sessions and pending redemptions from this workspace before escalating a mobile loyalty incident.

data.name: LoyaltyRedemptionPlaybookScanFailureScope
data.value: Failed or expired scan-linked redemptions usually indicate operator or device friction rather than loyalty-balance corruption.

data.name: MobilePlaybookPushDebtAction
data.value: Review missing-token and notifications-disabled lanes before assuming the problem is in loyalty rules or communication templates.

data.name: MobilePlaybookTransportAction
data.value: Check mobile bootstrap, admin alerts, and communication readiness together before treating a support case as app-only drift.

data.name: MobilePlaybookProviderLaneScope
data.value: Scan and alert incidents can come from transport pressure rather than app drift, especially when SMS or WhatsApp verification overlaps with mobile support.

data.name: MobilePlaybookProviderLaneAction
data.value: Open communication provider review before escalating an app incident when verification, alerts, or scan follow-up look transport-linked.

data.name: NoDevicesMatchCurrentFilters
data.value: No devices match the current filters.

data.name: LoyaltyProgramPlaybookSpendScope
data.value: Spend-based programs tie loyalty balances more tightly to orders, refunds, and financial support workflows.

data.name: LoyaltyProgramPlaybookReadinessAction
data.value: Keep one clear active program path per business, and review inactive or missing-rule programs before go-live support escalates.

data.name: MobilePlaybookPushDebtScope
data.value: Missing push tokens and disabled notifications usually explain why loyalty campaigns or mobile alerts appear inconsistent across businesses.

data.name: LoyaltyRewardTierPlaybookSelfAction
data.value: Prioritize self-redemption tiers when investigating unexpected redemption flow or scanner support issues.

data.name: LoyaltyAccountPlaybookZeroBalanceAction
data.value: Check recent accrual activity and the active loyalty program before escalating a balance complaint.

data.name: LoyaltyScanPlaybookPendingAction
data.value: Review pending sessions before retrying campaigns or loyalty settings so device-side drift is separated from program drift.

data.name: LoyaltyAccountPlaybookSuspendedAction
data.value: Review the recent transaction and redemption trail first, then reactivate only after the underlying cause is clear.

data.name: LoyaltyProgramPlaybookReadinessScope
data.value: Inactive programs and rule-light programs are the first place to review when accrual or redemption expectations drift between mobile, staff, and admin support.

data.name: LoyaltyRewardTierPlaybookCatalogAction
data.value: Fill in descriptions and verify reward type intent before operators, campaigns, or storefront messaging rely on the catalog.

data.name: LoyaltyRedemptionPlaybookScanFailureAction
data.value: Read the scan outcome first, then decide whether the case needs account review, scanner review, or reward-catalog cleanup.

data.name: LoyaltyRedemptionPlaybookMobileScope
data.value: Pending or failed redemptions often need device-state and notification review, not only account or reward review.

data.name: LoyaltyRedemptionPlaybookMobileAction
data.value: Check the member in Mobile Operations and then return to the redemption or account queue with the device context in hand.

data.name: LoyaltyAccountPlaybookSuspendedScope
data.value: Suspended accounts usually indicate unresolved support or fraud-safe follow-up before loyalty earning and redemption can resume.

data.name: AddOnEditIntro
data.value: Keep add-on scope, active state, and option pricing aligned before widening a storefront issue.

data.name: AddOnPlaybookVariantScope
data.value: Variant-level add-ons create the most specific override path and are the first place to inspect when only one SKU behaves differently.

data.name: AddOnPlaybookScopeScope
data.value: Global groups and unattached groups affect whether add-ons show everywhere, nowhere, or only on targeted catalog branches.

data.name: AddOnGroupConcurrencyConflict
data.value: Concurrency conflict: the record was modified by another user.

data.name: AddOnPlaybookVariantAction
data.value: Inspect variant-linked groups before widening an issue to the whole product, category, or brand.

data.name: AddOnPlaybookScopeAction
data.value: Check scope and attachment level before blaming storefront rendering or product configuration.

data.name: AddOnPlaybookInactiveScope
data.value: Inactive add-on groups often explain why expected upsell choices never appear on products or in checkout.

data.name: AddOnOptionsEditorHelp
data.value: Use options like Size or Sauce and values like Large or Extra spicy. Values may add or subtract price through minor currency units.

data.name: AddOnCreateIntro
data.value: Define the shared option structure, pricing deltas, and scope before attaching the group across catalog surfaces.

data.name: AddOnSearchVariants
data.value: Search by SKU or product name...

data.name: AddOnPlaybookInactiveAction
data.value: Review inactive groups before product-level debugging so merchandising and pricing support stay aligned.

data.name: AddOnPlaybookUnattachedScope
data.value: Configured add-on groups with no assignments cannot affect product configuration in checkout or ordering flows.

data.name: AddOnPlaybookGlobalAction
data.value: Confirm the scope is really global and review downstream product, category, and brand expectations before editing.

data.name: AddOnPlaybookGlobalScope
data.value: Global add-on groups can affect broad portions of the catalog and are higher-risk configuration changes.

data.name: AddOnPlaybookUnattachedAction
data.value: Attach the group to variants, products, categories, or brands depending on the intended scope.

data.name: AddOnPlaybookInactiveScopeLive
data.value: Inactive groups stay visible in admin but should not surprise operators expecting them in live product configuration.

data.name: AddOnPlaybookInactiveActionLive
data.value: Review whether the group is intentionally retired or should be reactivated before business-side menu operations.

data.name: PagesPlaybookLiveWindowScope
data.value: A page currently live inside a publish window is timing-sensitive and worth proactive review.

data.name: PageTranslationRequired
data.value: At least one translation is required.

data.name: PageCreateIntro
data.value: Create the storefront page, then complete translations and publish timing deliberately.

data.name: PageContentHelpNote
data.value: Rich text content rendered on the page.

data.name: PagesPlaybookDraftScope
data.value: Draft pages usually mean unfinished legal, help, onboarding, or campaign content is still waiting on an owner.

data.name: PageConcurrencyConflict
data.value: The page was changed by another operator. Reload it before saving again.

data.name: PagesPlaybookWindowedAction
data.value: Review publish start and end dates and confirm they still match campaign or compliance expectations.

data.name: PageMetaDescriptionHelpContent
data.value: Short SEO description, ideally around 155 characters or less.

data.name: PageSlugHelpContent
data.value: URL-friendly identifier. Use lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens. Example: about-us.

data.name: PagesPlaybookWindowedScope
data.value: Windowed pages can silently expire or go live at the wrong time when campaign timing drifts.

data.name: PageContentHelpContent
data.value: Use headings, lists, and links deliberately. Media should support the publish intent and translation context.

data.name: PagesWorkspaceIntro
data.value: Operate legal, help, and marketing content with publish-window awareness and translation follow-up.

data.name: PagesPlaybookLiveWindowAction
data.value: Verify content accuracy, links, and expiry timing while the page is actively visible.

data.name: PageTranslationsHelp
data.value: Keep storefront wording, SEO metadata, and publish timing aligned across cultures.

data.name: SearchPagesPlaceholder
data.value: Search title, slug, or translation content

data.name: PagesPlaybookDraftAction
data.value: Open the page, finish the translations, and publish only after the responsible owner confirms the content.

data.name: PageEditIntro
data.value: Review translation coverage, publishing windows, and content accuracy before going live.

data.name: CrmInvoicesPlaybookRefundScope
data.value: Refund activity can drift away from invoice status, payment settlement, and customer communication if it is not reviewed.

data.name: CrmInvoicesPlaybookRefundAction
data.value: Verify invoice status, refunded amount, related payment trail, and the follow-up communication before closing the case.

data.name: CrmInvoicesPlaybookVatGapAction
data.value: Open the customer profile, capture the VAT identifier, and review whether reverse-charge handling still applies.

data.name: CrmInvoicesPlaybookVatGapScope
data.value: A business tax profile without a VAT identifier creates invoicing and compliance cleanup later.

data.name: CrmInvoicesPlaybookDueSoonScope
data.value: Outstanding invoices close to their due date can turn into support debt and collection churn quickly.

data.name: CrmInvoicesPlaybookDueSoonAction
data.value: Review payment linkage, follow up with the customer, and confirm the due date still matches the commercial agreement.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookReconciliationDependency
data.value: Stripe secret key, webhook secret, and merchant identity should be configured before operators rely on these rows as trustworthy reconciliation evidence.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookHistoryAction
data.value: Check provider reference state, last event timestamp, and linked refund activity before deciding whether a row is settled, stale, or missing follow-up.

data.name: BillingRefundsPlaybookFailedDependency
data.value: Webhook and callback audit depth is still near-term, so operator review remains the source of truth for failed refund follow-up.

data.name: BillingRefundsPlaybookPendingScope
data.value: Use this queue for refunds that have been requested but are not yet operationally settled.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookReconciliationAction
data.value: Start from the payment row, verify provider reference, refund totals, and linked order or invoice context, then document the final state in the payment editor or support queue.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookFailedDependency
data.value: Webhook secret and provider credentials must be configured before trusting automated Stripe lifecycle handling.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookDisputeScope
data.value: Use this subset for Stripe rows that should be cross-checked against dispute signals instead of being treated as routine refund noise.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookPendingScope
data.value: Use this for payments waiting on capture or manual completion.

data.name: BillingRefundsPlaybookHistoryScope
data.value: Use provider reference state and last refund event to distinguish stale support markers from real provider-side movement.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookFailedScope
data.value: Treat these as support items first, not silent retries.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookHistoryScope
data.value: Use provider reference state and last financial event to spot rows whose provider trail is stale or incomplete.

data.name: BillingRefundsPlaybookHistoryAction
data.value: Check provider reference state, open-age window, and linked payment before deciding whether the refund is in flight, failed, or simply missing provider correlation.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookFailedAction
data.value: Open the linked order or customer, verify provider reference or failure state, and only add or edit payments after support validation.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookMissingProviderAction
data.value: Open the payment or linked order, verify the real Stripe intent or charge id, and correct the provider reference before treating the record as complete.

data.name: BillingRefundsPlaybookFailedAction
data.value: Start from the refund row, then move into the linked payment workbench or order refund flow to correct support notes and retry paths manually.

data.name: BillingRefundsPlaybookHistoryDependency
data.value: Stripe webhook secret and provider reference hygiene should be green before these timeline signals are treated as reliable evidence.

data.name: BillingRefundsPlaybookCompletedAction
data.value: Review linked payment, invoice, and order settlement together when customers ask about refunded value or net collected totals.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookMissingProviderDependency
data.value: Stripe webhook secret and secret key should be configured before assuming provider-linked automation is trustworthy.

data.name: RefundWebhookTriageNote
data.value: Refund triage may depend on callback-level evidence. Use these subsets when Stripe refund or charge behavior looks inconsistent with the queue row alone.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookPendingDependency
data.value: Stripe secret key and merchant identity should already be configured before live capture workflows are relied on.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookPendingAction
data.value: Review the order and payment rows, then record or correct payment status from the payment editor when the provider outcome is known.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookLinkingAction
data.value: Link order or invoice context where known, or keep the row documented as a standalone support payment until Stripe-specific reconciliation matures.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookHistoryDependency
data.value: Stripe webhook secret and provider credentials should be in place before operators treat these timeline signals as trustworthy settlement history.

data.name: BillingRefundsPlaybookPendingDependency
data.value: Stripe webhook and provider references should be trusted before using pending state as anything more than a manual support marker.

data.name: BillingTaxReadinessNote
data.value: This phase-1 tax surface improves billing visibility for VAT defaults and invoice issuer identity without claiming a full tax engine or e-invoicing completion.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookReconciliationScope
data.value: Use this subset for pending, failed, refunded, or Stripe-reference-light rows that still need operator confirmation before finance treats them as settled.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookDisputeAction
data.value: Open the dispute-follow-up subset, pivot into webhook dispute signals, then decide whether the next step is payment correction, refund follow-up, or escalation.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookDisputeDependency
data.value: Stripe webhook lifecycle visibility and payment settings should be configured before callback anomalies are treated as resolved.

data.name: BillingRefundsPlaybookPendingAction
data.value: Open the linked order and payment, confirm the provider-side outcome, and leave the refund pending only when support expects a later completion signal.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookLinkingScope
data.value: These rows usually represent reconciliation or data-hygiene work.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookMissingProviderScope
data.value: These rows are risky because callback and reconciliation correlation is weak.

data.name: BillingPaymentsPlaybookLinkingDependency
data.value: Phase-1 Stripe readiness should be green before treating provider-linked rows as production-grade evidence.

data.name: BillingRefundsPlaybookCompletedDependency
data.value: Stripe readiness and provider reference hygiene should already be green before these rows are treated as reconciliation evidence.

data.name: BillingRefundsPlaybookCompletedScope
data.value: These rows represent already recorded customer giveback and should stay aligned with payment and invoice context.

data.name: BillingWebhookLifecycleNote
data.value: This queue gives callback visibility for phase-1 payment operations. It is intentionally read-oriented and does not claim automated resend or full reconciliation completion.

data.name: BillingRefundsPlaybookFailedScope
data.value: Use this subset when refund status or Stripe handling needs explicit finance support review.

data.name: ShipmentQueueIntro
data.value: Cross-order shipment review with DHL readiness, handoff thresholds, tracking grace windows, and return follow-up guidance.

data.name: SearchShipmentsPlaceholder
data.value: Search by order, carrier, service, or tracking

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookPendingScope
data.value: Rows still pending or packed usually need warehouse allocation, label generation, or final handoff confirmation.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookPendingAction
data.value: Review the order shipment tab, confirm warehouse selection, and add a shipment record once the parcel is ready to leave.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookPendingDependency
data.value: Shipping methods, warehouse assignment, and allocation settings drive this queue.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookAwaitingHandoffScope
data.value: These parcels were created but have been open longer than the configured handoff threshold.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookAwaitingHandoffAction
data.value: Check carrier pickup status, warehouse packing progress, and raise follow-up if the parcel is still physically on site.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookAwaitingHandoffDependency
data.value: Shipment attention delay in shipping settings determines when this queue opens.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookTrackingOverdueScope
data.value: Shipped parcels without fresh tracking events beyond the grace window need carrier-side verification.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookTrackingOverdueAction
data.value: Review carrier events, confirm the tracking number is valid, and escalate to the carrier if no movement is reported.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookTrackingOverdueDependency
data.value: Tracking grace in shipping settings controls when these rows become overdue.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookMissingTrackingScope
data.value: Rows without a tracking number break downstream customer support and return handling.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookMissingTrackingAction
data.value: Open the order shipment tab, add the missing tracking reference, and verify the carrier/service pairing is correct.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookMissingTrackingDependency
data.value: Carrier/service data and operational handoff discipline both affect this queue.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookDhlDataTitle
data.value: DHL rows with incomplete carrier data

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookDhlDataScope
data.value: DHL-tagged rows without service or complete shipper readiness usually indicate shipping configuration drift.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookDhlDataAction
data.value: Review DHL settings, confirm service mapping, and fix missing carrier metadata before the next shipment is created.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookDhlDataDependency
data.value: DHL site settings and shipping method definitions are the primary dependencies.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookCarrierReviewScope
data.value: Carrier review rows combine missing service, stale tracking, or return-oriented exceptions that need manual assessment.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookCarrierReviewAction
data.value: Use the order timeline and carrier events to decide whether the issue is a data fix, a provider escalation, or a return follow-up.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookCarrierReviewDependency
data.value: Shipping settings, carrier data completeness, and return support rules all influence this queue.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookReturnedScope
data.value: Returned parcels need coordinated review across shipment status, refunds, and support notes.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookReturnedAction
data.value: Open the order refund flow, confirm the return reason, and make sure the payment path still allows the intended refund.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookReturnedDependency
data.value: Refund availability and payment linkage determine whether a return can be closed cleanly.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookReturnFollowUpScope
data.value: These rows already look return-related and should be triaged before they turn into billing or support drift.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookReturnFollowUpAction
data.value: Review the order, open refunds if needed, and confirm the return status is reflected in shipment, payment, and support context.

data.name: ShipmentPlaybookReturnFollowUpDependency
data.value: Return support flow, refund workflows, and carrier updates all matter here.

data.name: SiteSettingsOwnershipIntro
data.value: Use site settings only for platform-wide defaults, provider credentials, and shared policy. Business-specific branding, sender identity, local defaults, and communication enablement belong in business setup.

data.name: SiteSettingsLocalizationDefaultsOwned
data.value: Fallback culture, currency, time zone, supported cultures

data.name: SiteSettingsLocalizationDefaultsBusiness
data.value: Per-business culture, currency, and time zone overrides

data.name: SiteSettingsLocalizationDefaultsAction
data.value: Adjust final business defaults from business setup after changing platform fallbacks.

data.name: SiteSettingsCommunicationPolicyOwned
data.value: SMTP/SMS/WhatsApp providers, admin routing, subject/body templates, test targets

data.name: SiteSettingsCommunicationPolicyBusiness
data.value: Sender name, reply-to, support email, channel enablement flags

data.name: SiteSettingsCommunicationPolicyAction
data.value: Use Business Setup and Business Communications for business-level readiness and flow support.

data.name: SiteSettingsPaymentsShippingOwned
data.value: Stripe and DHL credentials, webhook/carrier thresholds, VAT and issuer defaults

data.name: SiteSettingsPaymentsShippingBusiness
data.value: Subscription plan choice, business-facing billing state, business communication around those flows

data.name: SiteSettingsPaymentsShippingAction
data.value: Use business subscription/support surfaces for tenant-specific triage after platform credentials are ready.

data.name: SiteSettingsBrandingDefaultsOwned
data.value: Leave brand display name, logo, support email, sender defaults, and channel toggles in business setup.

data.name: SiteSettingsBrandingDefaultsBusiness
data.value: Business setup and communication readiness drive the final customer-facing defaults.

data.name: SiteSettingsBrandingDefaultsAction
data.value: Do not duplicate these values globally unless they are true platform defaults.

data.name: SiteSettingsLocalizationIntro
data.value: Platform localization owns fallback culture, supported cultures, shared date/time formatting, and the default currency/country baseline. Tenant-specific wording and day-to-day customer-facing defaults still belong in business setup and CRM support follow-up.

data.name: SiteSettingsFallbackGovernanceNote
data.value: Use these fields when a culture, currency, or time-zone default should change for every new business or for platform-wide fallback behavior.

data.name: SiteSettingsBusinessOverridePathNote
data.value: If only one tenant needs a different culture, currency, or time zone, keep the override in business setup instead of mutating the platform fallback.

data.name: SiteSettingsFallbackRuleNote
data.value: Fallback rule: keep SupportedCulturesCsv aligned with what admin and customer surfaces can actually render. Add a business-level override only when one tenant must differ from the platform default; otherwise update the global fallback here and review tenant defaults from Business Setup.

data.name: CommunicationSubjectPrefixNote
data.value: Applied to the current invitation, activation, and password-reset email subjects.

data.name: CommunicationTestInboxNote
data.value: When configured, current transactional emails are redirected here and include the original recipient in the body.

data.name: CommunicationTestEmailReadyNote
data.value: A test email can be sent safely from this workspace because SMTP and the test inbox are both configured.

data.name: CommunicationTestEmailBlockedNote
data.value: Configure both SMTP and the communication test inbox to enable a safe operator-side test email action.

data.name: CommunicationTestSmsTargetNote
data.value: Used for operator-side SMS transport tests before live rollout.

data.name: CommunicationTestSmsReadyNote
data.value: A safe operator-side SMS test is available because transport and test target are configured.

data.name: CommunicationTestWhatsAppTargetNote
data.value: Used for operator-side WhatsApp transport tests before live rollout.

data.name: CommunicationTestWhatsAppReadyNote
data.value: A safe operator-side WhatsApp test is available because transport and test target are configured.

data.name: SiteSettingsWhatsAppEnabledHelp
data.value: Enable WhatsApp integration to send notifications via the WhatsApp Business Cloud API.

data.name: SiteSettingsWebAuthnRpIdHelp
data.value: Registrable domain (e.g., example.com) or 'localhost' for dev.

data.name: SiteSettingsWebAuthnUserVerificationHelp
data.value: When enabled, requires biometric/PIN verification on authenticators.

data.name: SiteSettingsCommunicationTestSmsTargetHelp
data.value: Reserved test target for SMS rollout and staged provider validation.

data.name: SiteSettingsPhoneVerificationPreferredChannelHelp
data.value: Default channel when Web or Mobile does not explicitly request one.

data.name: SiteSettingsPhoneVerificationPolicyNote
data.value: Phone verification policy: if the client does not specify a channel, the platform uses the preferred channel above. If fallback is enabled, the system may switch between SMS and WhatsApp when the preferred or requested channel is unavailable.

data.name: SiteSettingsCommunicationTestEmailSubjectHelp
data.value: Subject used for operator-side diagnostic email tests.

data.name: SiteSettingsCommunicationTestEmailBodyHelp
data.value: HTML body used for diagnostic email tests to the configured test inbox.

data.name: SiteSettingsCommunicationTestSmsTemplateHelp
data.value: Plain-text SMS used for operator-side SMS provider validation.

data.name: SiteSettingsCommunicationTestWhatsAppTemplateHelp
data.value: Plain-text WhatsApp message used for operator-side WhatsApp validation.

data.name: SiteSettingsPhoneVerificationSmsTemplateHelp
data.value: Plain-text SMS template for current-user mobile verification.

data.name: SiteSettingsPhoneVerificationWhatsAppTemplateHelp
data.value: Plain-text WhatsApp template for current-user mobile verification.

data.name: BusinessesRequiringEmailSetupNote
data.value: Businesses with email-related toggles enabled but missing support or sender defaults.

data.name: MissingSupportEmailNote
data.value: Counts businesses that still have no business-level support mailbox configured.

data.name: MissingSenderIdentityNote
data.value: Counts businesses missing sender name or reply-to defaults.

data.name: ChannelAttempts24hNote
data.value: Recent SMS and WhatsApp sends for verification and operator diagnostics.

data.name: SmsWhatsAppMixNote
data.value: Cross-channel activity mix for currently live non-email flows.

data.name: PhoneVerificationActivityNote
data.value: Delivery attempts generated by the canonical mobile-number verification flow.

data.name: ChannelFailuresNote
data.value: Operator-visible non-email delivery failures that need transport or policy review.

data.name: ChannelAuditBaselineNote
data.value: This is the current multi-channel audit baseline. It covers the live SMS and WhatsApp families we already use for phone verification and operator diagnostics, without pretending a full outbox or generic notification bus exists yet.

data.name: CommunicationTestWhatsAppSentMessage
data.value: Communication test WhatsApp message sent to {0}.

data.name: SearchWebhookDeliveriesPlaceholder
data.value: Search event type, callback, status, or idempotency key

data.name: WebhookDisputeEscalationNote
data.value: Manual finance escalation only; no dedicated dispute workflow exists yet.

data.name: WebhookPaymentEvidenceNote
data.value: Treat as payment-callback evidence, then confirm the linked payment/refund queue.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookPendingScope
data.value: Treat pending webhook rows as lifecycle visibility, not proof that payment automation has completed.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookPendingAction
data.value: Review event type, callback target, and attempt timeline before assuming Stripe-side updates have reached the platform.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookPendingDependency
data.value: Stripe webhook secret and payment settings should be configured before pending rows are used as operational evidence.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookFailedScope
data.value: Use this queue when provider callback correlation is weak or callback endpoints are unhealthy.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookFailedAction
data.value: Review callback URL, response code, retry count, and the linked payment/provider reference, then escalate through settings or infrastructure rather than editing finance records blindly.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookFailedDependency
data.value: Webhook endpoint registration and Stripe secret must be correct before failed deliveries are treated as application bugs.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookRetryPendingScope
data.value: These rows show callbacks that have already had at least one failed/manual retry path.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookRetryPendingAction
data.value: Use them to prioritize reconciliation review and confirm whether payment/refund state must be corrected manually in WebAdmin.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookRetryPendingDependency
data.value: Phase-1 still has no automated resend daemon, so operator review remains the source of truth.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookPaymentExceptionsScope
data.value: Use this subset for payment_intent, charge, and refund callbacks whose status is still failed, pending, or retry-heavy.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookPaymentExceptionsAction
data.value: Start from the callback row, verify the event type and retry trail, then move into the payment or refund queue before treating the finance record as settled.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookPaymentExceptionsDependency
data.value: Stripe secret key and webhook secret must be configured before these rows are used as trustworthy provider exception evidence.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookDisputeSignalsScope
data.value: This is a phase-1 callback-only proxy for charge dispute or chargeback-like events. It is not a complete dispute workflow.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookDisputeSignalsAction
data.value: Use the callback row to identify the affected provider signal, then move into payment/refund follow-up and document manual escalation because no dedicated dispute aggregate exists yet.

data.name: WebhookPlaybookDisputeSignalsDependency
data.value: Webhook registration and payment settings must be healthy before operators interpret dispute-like events as application issues.

data.name: CommunicationOpsDashboardIntro
data.value: Phase-1 email/SMS/WhatsApp readiness across platform and business defaults.

data.name: CommunicationRetriedDeliveryChainsNote
data.value: Recent audit rows that are part of a repeated delivery chain for the same flow and recipient.

data.name: CommunicationPriorSuccessContextNote
data.value: Recent audit rows where the same flow and recipient already had a successful delivery before.

data.name: CommunicationRepeatedFailureChainsNote
data.value: Recent follow-up rows that are not first-time failures and likely need deeper operator review.

data.name: CommunicationCurrentTransactionalFlowsNote
data.value: This is a phase-1 visibility catalog. These flows are operational, but they still use direct transactional composition and do not yet provide a full template engine, delivery log, retry queue, or reusable Communication Core workflow.

data.name: CommunicationTemplateInventoryNote
